channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: Crude Stabilizes After Sharp Selloff
item.description: Oil edged higher, but prices remain under pressure as a US-Iran peace deal could restore major supplies to the market.

item.title: Iraq Readies to Boost Oil Exports When Hormuz Reopens
item.description: There are already signs of shipping activity, with Iran moving its own oil tankers and other vessels changing course to head toward the Persian Gulf even before Washington and Tehran sign an interim agreement to end…

item.title: CLNE to Deliver LNG Systems for 2 Power Projects in Puerto Rico
item.description: Clean Energy Fuels won contracts to design and install liquefied natural gas fuel systems for 2 power projects in Puerto Rico by PR Energy Partners and an unnamed international healthcare manufacturer.

item.title: TotalEnergies Doubled Oil Trading Profit in Q1
item.description: TotalEnergies's oil trading profits doubled to about $1 billion in the first quarter as the energy giant went on a crude buying spree in the run-up to the Iran war, its CEO said.

item.title: Iberdrola Raises $1.7B from Debt Instrument Sale
item.description: 'The proceeds will be used to finance network investments in Iberdrola's core markets, as well as to refinance selected renewable energy projects'.

item.title: Former BP CEO Looney Joins Board of Fusion Power Developer
item.description: Type One Energy named Bernard Looney, who resigned as BP chief executive in 2023 as he admitted failing to sufficiently disclose personal relationships with colleagues, to its board of directors.

item.title: DFC, I Squared to Form $3B Platform to Expand US Energy Reach in Asia
item.description: The platform 'will help strengthen energy connectivity between the United States and the Indo-Pacific region, including expanded access to U.S. LNG and related energy exports'.

item.title: Iran to Get Major Economic Relief under US Deal
item.description: Iran would be allowed to start oil exports immediately under an interim deal with the U.S. and gain access to a $300 billion economic development program following negotiations for a permanent peace meant to address…

item.title: Oil Extends Losses as Hormuz Reopens
item.description: Oil fell for a fourth straight session as a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz improved expectations for increased crude supply.

item.title: Chinese Refining in May at Lowest Level in Nearly 4 Years
item.description: The question for the market, now that a deal has been reached to reopen Hormuz, is whether Chinese demand for fuels like gasoline will normalize, or whether the rapid transport electrification has permanently dented…

item.title: Europe Wary of Sending Hormuz Aid as Questions Linger
item.description: At the Group of Seven Summit, held in France, Europeans have raised a series of questions about what exactly was agreed to before committing to demining missions and patrols.

item.title: Hundreds of Well Workers offshore Norway Go on Strike
item.description: The Norwegian Union of Energy Workers is staging strikes against offshore well services companies after refusing proposals that the union sees as years behind collective bargaining agreements in other parts of the oil…

item.title: USA EIA Raises 2026, 2027 Henry Hub Price Forecasts
item.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration raised its Henry Hub spot price forecasts for 2026 and 2027 in its latest short term energy outlook.

item.title: Iberdrola to Invest Nearly $5B in Brazilian State of Bahia by 2030
item.description: 'Through its subsidiary Neoenergia Coelba, the company will build 54 new substations, more than 2,000 kilometers of high-voltage transmission lines and 42,000 kilometers of medium-voltage distribution networks'.

item.title: Chevron Expands Exploration Footprint in Greek Mediterranean
item.description: Chevron and Helleniq Energy have now expanded their partnership in Greece to five offshore concessions.
